narrative: Saturday morning after the storm. Ten inches on the ground, sky scrubbed clean, the sun so bright on the snow it hurts to look at. The plows have been through the main roads but the side streets are still buried. Cole is out checking the ranch at first light and finds the dog at the east gate — some kind of shepherd mix, thin, shaking, planted in the snow like it chose this spot and intends to hold it. He leaves a bowl of food and goes about his morning, checking back every twenty minutes. On I-90, a jackknifed semi near Laurel has traffic backed up for miles, and the detour routes everyone through downtown, which on a Saturday morning means the coffee shop has a line out the door and Amber, who came in early to assess the pipe damage in better light, can hear the unusual traffic from inside the store. Ryan has his kids this weekend and they’re building something in the backyard that started as a snow fort and has become an engineering project.
events: Cole finds a stray dog at the ranch gate — ribs showing, no collar, won’t let him get closer than ten feet but won’t leave either
A car accident on I-90 near the Laurel interchange detours Saturday morning traffic through downtown
